evidence_quotes: I was stunned. I told the representative that before with the last fraud I was told 'bank of America would NEVER call' but now you are saying they will?! How was I supposed to know it was not the real bank of America last time since the same thing happened last time and I was robbed $5500.00.

I asked multiple times to speak to a manager or someone above him. He stated that there was no one else and the only thing I could do was call the same number back but that I would get the same type of representative that was only allowed to give the same information.

I have been with Bank of America for 15 years. I told him how this is a crazy amount of money to me and people kill themselves for less and this is how bank of America chooses to treat their people! He then told me he misunderstood and that he was trying to say I couldn't speak to an analyst but that he could get a manager on the line. I told him that I knew from before I couldn't speak to an analyst.

I have NEVER wired money from my bank of America account. Why didn't they stop that as suspicious activity? I have been a bank of America customer for 15 years and never had this happen. I have multiple different accounts with different banks and never had someone call impersonating my bank. When I checked CFPB's site, Bank of America has 336 cases for fraud from the last 6 months and over 10 of the cases that were public are exactly like my claim. They also received calls and texts from bank of America impersonators. They suffered the SAME HUGE LOSSES from wire transfers. Bank of America made them the SAME PROMISES they made me. Told me the same things which turned out to be all lies.
